description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular, stacked-stone wall forming the main body, set on a square patch of grassy ground. The well is topped by a pitched, reddish-brown wooden roof supported by two vertical wooden beams, providing shelter from above. A wooden crank handle is attached to one side, connected to a spindle and rope mechanism for drawing water, though no bucket is visible. The stonework appears weathered and mossy, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The roof is constructed from overlapping wooden shingles, and the overall structure is robust and utilitarian. The well is open at the top, allowing access to the interior shaft.
description: A mossy stone water well with a pitched wooden roof and a crank handle, set on a grassy base. The structure is open-topped and features weathered, natural materials.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pitched roof, stone well, and wooden support beams, with the crank handle not visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the crank handle and spindle mechanism on the right, with the open well mouth and roof shingles clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the pitched roof, stone wall, and support beams, with the crank mechanism partially visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the open well, roof, and support beams, with the crank handle on the left side.
attribute_well_cover_design: Pitched wooden roof with overlapping shingles, supported by two vertical beams; no lid or grating over the well opening.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: Wooden crank handle and spindle with rope for drawing water; no bucket visible.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately waist-high, with a wooden roof canopy above.
